_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, averaging prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with 2015 as the base year (index=100), currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase since 2015. This headline rise has been uneven across the components and largely driven by recent shifts in particular commodities. The basket’s increase does not reflect uniform price inflation but rather varied movements within a small set of key soft commodities. These commodities represent thin markets where prices can fluctuate sharply due to specific factors.
notes.movers: Among the basket items, coffee has experienced the largest price increase at +93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at +22%. In contrast, wheat and sugar have seen relatively modest gains of 7% and 5%, respectively. The main drivers are weather-related and disease-related supply disruptions — for example, frost and drought in coffee-growing arabica regions and citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice crops. These idiosyncratic shocks in small, specialized markets, rather than broad-based inflation, have largely powered the basket’s change.
notes.why: A few weather-sensitive crops, with limited production areas and susceptible to disease, lead price moves in this segment of global commodities. Because these underlying risks are complex and persistent, elevated prices for these inputs may remain sustained over extended periods. Consequently, the basket’s headline rise reflects supply-side volatility rather than general inflationary pressures.
